briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. Welcome to the climate briefing for April 30, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Change Crisis and Widespread Impacts
sections.content: Today's news reveals that the global threat posed by climate change is intensifying. Over the past 40 years, mountain snow has decreased by nearly 60% [30, 31, 45, 47, 49, 51, 54, 55, 56, 59], and warnings have emerged that climate change, through extreme weather and pollution, can increase the risk of stroke and impede cancer treatment [25, 26, 58]. Furthermore, climate change is affecting Cabernet grape cultivation [20], accelerating the spread of invasive species in Alaskan salmon habitats [36], and confronting nations like Vanuatu with an unimaginable future [21, 38, 43]. In particular, water scarcity is deepening in Africa [50], and Europe's warming rate is outpacing the global trend [46], positioning the climate crisis as a new front in global security [35].

sections.title: ⚠️ Policy Challenges and the Duality of Climate Action
sections.content: Alongside policy efforts to address the climate crisis, challenges and dualistic attitudes are also being observed in some countries. In the United States, Democrats criticized the proposed budget cuts for the head of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) as a 'climate change denial manifesto' [22, 60], and the U.S. was notably not invited to climate change discussions involving various countries worldwide [23]. Domestically, a phenomenon where 'carbon neutrality' is being overlooked in pledges has emerged [7], leading to calls for speaking out against policies that exacerbate climate change [27]. This reality contrasts with the firm stance of European leaders that renegotiating climate change agreements is impossible [52], demonstrating how challenging it is to consistently pursue climate action.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Action and Efforts to Strengthen Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts are actively underway to respond to climate change and accelerate the transition to a carbon-neutral society. Jeju Gosan Global Atmosphere Watch Station is continuing its long-term record of climate change by compiling the 'Climate Change Annals' [1]. The Korea Environmental Preservation Agency, private company Daangn, and the Ministry of Climate have signed a business agreement to 'spread a culture of carbon neutrality practice' and are leading public awareness campaigns [2, 4, 5, 6, 9]. The Ministry of Climate launched the 'Carbon Neutrality and Energy Transition Korea Climate Action Preparatory Committee' to establish a focal point for nationwide climate action [8], and the Climate Change Center is proposing urban climate response solutions through its garden city strategy [3]. Korea South-East Power Co., Ltd. (KOEN) is also participating in climate action through the selection of government-supported projects and carbon-neutral lifestyle practice campaigns [10].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Technological Innovation
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy and eco-friendly technological innovation are acting as key drivers in responding to the climate crisis. Domestically, renewable energy installed capacity has entered the 100 GW era [12], and plans to include thermal energy such as air-source and geothermal heat within the scope of renewable energy are being pursued, which will accelerate energy security and the decarbonization transition [11, 15, 17]. Internationally, India is expanding its renewable energy investments with a 500 GW target [14], and the EBRD is making large-scale investments in renewable energy projects in Egypt and Hungary [16], demonstrating the global acceleration of the clean energy transition [33, 41]. At the Colombia Climate Conference and the Santa Marta Summit, support for country-specific roadmaps to cease fossil fuel production and use was expressed [61, 63], and China also reported 'strict control' over fossil fuels and a surge in clean technology exports [64]. Furthermore, LG Energy Solution signed a large-scale electric vehicle battery supply contract with BMW [18], and biodegradable plastics utilizing discarded fallen leaves are being developed [19], with such innovative technologies contributing to overcoming the climate crisis [37, 42, 62].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severe reality of climate change, along with diverse domestic and international efforts to overcome it, and the policy challenges that still persist. This is a time when continuous attention and action become even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: LG Energy Solution Signs '10 Trillion Won' Battery Contract with BMW
articles.original_title: LG엔솔, BMW와 ‘10조원’ 배터리 계약
articles.description: LG Energy Solution reportedly secures 100GWh cylindrical battery order for EVs. LG Energy Solution is said to have signed a 10 trillion won electric vehicle battery supply contract with German premium automaker BMW. This follows Samsung SDI's initial agreement to supply EV batteries to Mercedes-Benz, drawing attention to whether the South Korean secondary battery industry, which faced an 'EV chasm' (demand cliff), can rebound.

summary.phenomenon: LG Energy Solution reportedly signed a 10 trillion won contract on the 30th to supply 100GWh of cylindrical batteries for electric vehicles to Germany's BMW.
summary.outlook: This contract draws attention to whether the South Korean secondary battery industry, which has faced an 'EV chasm' (demand cliff), can rebound.

articles.title: Discarded Fallen Leaves Reborn as Biodegradable Plastic Film
articles.original_title: 버려진 낙엽, 생분해성 비닐로 재탄생
articles.description: KAIST Develops 'Eco-friendly Mulching Film' - Approximately 34% decomposed in 115 days underground. KAIST researchers have developed a biodegradable agricultural film using fallen leaves to replace plastic films that cause soil pollution. Professor Myung Jae-wook's team announced on the 30th that they developed an eco-friendly mulching film that decomposes underground using fallen leaves.

summary.phenomenon: A research team led by Professor Myung Jae-wook from KAIST's Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering has developed a biodegradable agricultural mulching film utilizing fallen leaves. This film has been confirmed to decompose by approximately 34% in 115 days underground.
summary.cause: This film was developed to replace conventional plastic films that cause soil pollution, and it uses fallen leaves as its raw material.
summary.outlook: This eco-friendly mulching film is expected to reduce soil pollution and contribute to sustainable agriculture.

articles.original_description: 카이스트 개발 ‘친환경 멀칭 필름’땅속에서 115일 만에 약 34% 분해카이스트 연구진이 낙엽을 이용해 토양 오염을 유발하는 플라스틱 비닐을 대체할 수 있는 생분해성 농업용 비닐을 개발했다. 카이스트는 명재욱 건설및환경공학과 교수 연구팀이 낙엽을 활용해 땅속에서 분해되는 친환경 멀칭 필름을 개발했다고 30일 밝혔다. 멀칭 필름은 토양을 덮어 잡초를 억제하고···
